_note: Source of truth: agri integrate_10m.py BAND_OFFSETS. Order is the physical layout in cube_10m.npz. MUST stay byte-identical; family is editorial. Per-band `description` / `interpretation` / `pitfalls` / `references` / `scalar_keys` are advisory and may be revised without bumping the manifest CID — they live alongside the load-bearing offset/dims/tempo/privacy fields purely so `/v1/bands` can self-explain to agents.

bands.description: Tessera annual foundation embedding (128-D fp16). A learned multimodal vector that fuses Sentinel-2 optical, Sentinel-1 radar, and seasonality into one position-stable vector per cell per year. The default similarity surface for `/v1/find_similar` because it captures land-cover, structure, and recent change in one shot.
bands.interpretation: Compare via cosine similarity (or Hamming on the `geotessera.bin128` sign-bit sibling). Two cells with similarity > 0.85 are usually the same physical archetype (e.g. dense urban, mature mangrove, sparse savanna). Not directly interpretable per dimension — treat as opaque.
bands.pitfalls: Yearly cadence: a cell that flips from forest to clearing mid-year reads as the *annual mean* until the next vintage. Use `temporal_diff` or `phenology` to detect intra-year change.

bands.description: Overture Maps Foundation per-cell aggregate — counts, areas, heights, and class-frequency vectors derived from the four open Overture themes (buildings, places, transportation, addresses).
bands.units: mixed (see dimensions[]); log-scaled count fields are ln(1+n)
bands.interpretation: Treat as a structural fingerprint of the built environment: `buildings.count_log` separates settled from empty cells; `places.category_freq` distinguishes residential / commercial / industrial; `transportation.road_length_m_log` ≈ urbanity proxy.
bands.pitfalls: Coverage is uneven globally — Overture inherits from OSM + Microsoft + Meta footprints, so dense in NA / EU, sparser in Africa and rural Asia. A zero in `buildings.count` may mean 'no buildings' OR 'unmapped'; cross-check with `ghsl` or `population`.

dimensions.name: buildings.count_log
dimensions.description: ln(1 + building footprint count) inside the cell bbox.

dimensions.name: buildings.area_m2_log
dimensions.description: ln(1 + total building footprint area).

dimensions.name: buildings.height_mean
dimensions.description: Mean building height where the height attribute is present.

dimensions.name: buildings.height_p95
dimensions.description: 95th-percentile building height — captures tall outliers.

dimensions.name: buildings.class_freq[12]
dimensions.description: Frequency vector across the 12 Overture building classes (residential, commercial, industrial, …). Indices 4..16.

dimensions.name: places.count_log
dimensions.description: ln(1 + POI count).

dimensions.name: places.operating_open_pct
dimensions.description: Fraction of POIs whose `operating_status = open` at last release.

dimensions.name: places.category_freq[20]
dimensions.description: Frequency vector across the 20 Overture top-level place categories. Indices 18..38.

dimensions.name: transportation.road_length_m_log
dimensions.description: ln(1 + total road segment length in metres).

dimensions.name: transportation.class_freq[12]
dimensions.description: Frequency vector across the 12 Overture road classes (motorway, trunk, primary, …). Indices 39..51.

dimensions.name: addresses.count_log
dimensions.description: ln(1 + count of addressed entities).

dimensions.name: base.water_area_m2_log
dimensions.description: ln(1 + Overture base-theme water polygon area).

dimensions.name: base.land_use_diversity
dimensions.description: Shannon entropy across the cell's land-use classes (0 = single use, ln(n_classes) = perfectly mixed).

dimensions.name: reserved[10]
dimensions.description: Forward-compat slot reservation, indices 54..64. Currently zero-padded.

bands._source_note: Overture Maps Foundation per-cell aggregate vector. 64 dims: [0..1] buildings.count_log, [1..2] buildings.area_m2_log, [2..3] buildings.height_mean, [3..4] buildings.height_p95, [4..16] buildings.class_freq[12], [16..17] places.count_log, [17..18] places.operating_open_pct, [18..38] places.category_freq[20], [38..39] transportation.road_length_m_log, [39..51] transportation.class_freq[12], [51..52] addresses.count_log, [52..53] base.water_area_m2_log, [53..54] base.land_use_diversity, [54..64] reserved_within_overture[10]. Source: anonymous S3 overturemaps-us-west-2; pure-Rust parquet + WKB read.

bands.description: Surface air-quality scalars sourced from Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service (CAMS) via the Open-Meteo Air Quality API. Hourly forecasts at ~11 km native resolution, free with no API key. Coverage 2013-08 onward.
bands.units: see scalar_keys (mostly µg/m³ pollutant mass concentrations; AOD-550 is unitless)
bands.interpretation: PM2.5 / PM10 / NO2 / O3 are the canonical four pollutants used by US EPA and EEA AQI calculators — see `aqi_class@1` for a cite-able 'Good / Moderate / Unhealthy' classification. AOD-550 is column-integrated aerosol optical depth at 550 nm; values >0.4 indicate hazy conditions, >1.0 indicate dust storm or wildfire smoke. SO2 and CO are useful for pinpoint industrial / biomass burning sources.
bands.pitfalls: CAMS is a global model assimilating satellite + ground stations — local hot-spots (a single industrial stack, a busy intersection) won't appear at ~11 km. For US/EU regulatory monitoring use the local agency feed instead. Forecast horizon is +96h; older tslots fall back to ERA5-CAMS reanalysis with weaker recent coverage.

dimensions.name: pm25
dimensions.description: Particulate matter ≤2.5 µm aerodynamic diameter — the dominant health-relevant pollutant.

dimensions.name: pm10
dimensions.description: Particulate matter ≤10 µm — coarser dust + sea spray + pollen.

dimensions.name: no2
dimensions.description: Nitrogen dioxide — traffic + combustion marker. WHO 24-hour guideline 25 µg/m³.

dimensions.name: o3
dimensions.description: Surface ozone — secondary photochemical pollutant, peaks midday in summer downwind of NOx + VOCs.

dimensions.name: so2
dimensions.description: Sulphur dioxide — coal/biomass/volcanic marker. Spikes near heavy industry.

dimensions.name: co
dimensions.description: Carbon monoxide — incomplete combustion marker (vehicles, household burning, wildfires).

dimensions.name: aod_550
dimensions.description: Aerosol optical depth at 550 nm — column-integrated aerosol load. Unitless; >0.4 = hazy, >1 = severe.

bands._source_note: Pollutant scalars in µg/m³ — CAMS native units. AOD-550 is unitless. All seven exposed via the unified Open-Meteo air-quality endpoint, which avoids the CDS API key required by the upstream CAMS ECMWF endpoint. Hourly cadence; tempo:fast.

bands.description: Forward-compatibility reservation slot, currently zero-padded. Reserved for a future no-key foundation embedding to claim without shifting any subsequent offset.
bands.interpretation: Recall on this band returns Absence facts today. Do not include it in similarity queries.
bands._source_note: Foundation-embedding reservation slot, 505 dims (was 512 before air_quality carved 7 dims at offset 192). Currently unattested — kept zero-padded so subsequent band offsets stay byte-stable.

bands.description: Sentinel-2 L2A surface-reflectance scalars at 10 m. Cube reserves 10 fp16 slots for the canonical S2 band set used by the agriculture / land-cover community.
bands.units: reflectance × 10000 (Sentinel-2 L2A scaling); divide by 1e4 for unit reflectance
bands.interpretation: B04 (red, 665 nm) + B08 (NIR, 842 nm) feed NDVI; B11 + B12 (SWIR) feed NDMI/NDWI; B05/B06/B07 (red-edge) feed NDRE and chlorophyll proxies.
bands.pitfalls: Cloud, shadow, and snow contaminate L2A scenes. The default materializer picks scenes <40% cloud and `intersects: Point`, but per-pixel haze can still skew values. For agriculture-grade work, use `indices.ndvi` (already cloud-gated) instead of raw bands.

dimensions.name: B02
dimensions.description: Blue, 490 nm, 10 m native.

dimensions.name: B03
dimensions.description: Green, 560 nm, 10 m native.

dimensions.name: B04
dimensions.description: Red, 665 nm, 10 m native.

dimensions.name: B05
dimensions.description: Red-edge 1, 705 nm, 20 m native.

dimensions.name: B06
dimensions.description: Red-edge 2, 740 nm, 20 m native.

dimensions.name: B07
dimensions.description: Red-edge 3, 783 nm, 20 m native.

dimensions.name: B08
dimensions.description: NIR broad, 842 nm, 10 m native.

dimensions.name: B8A
dimensions.description: NIR narrow, 865 nm, 20 m native.

dimensions.name: B11
dimensions.description: SWIR 1, 1610 nm, 20 m native — water content / mineral / burn discrimination.

dimensions.name: B12
dimensions.description: SWIR 2, 2190 nm, 20 m native — geology / fire / SWIR-water indices.

bands.description: Sentinel-1 RTC (Radiometrically Terrain Corrected) C-band SAR backscatter in dB. Two slots (VV, VH) but only VV is currently materialized.
bands.interpretation: Higher values = more backscatter = rougher / wetter / more vertical-structured surface. Smooth water sits near −20 dB; bare soil −12 to −8; forest canopy −10 to −6; urban can exceed 0. Works at night and through cloud — the all-weather complement to `indices.ndvi`.
bands.pitfalls: VH slot is reserved but currently unattested. Treat sentinel1_raw as a 1-vector (VV-only) when no VH-bearing attestation is present at the cell. Speckle noise is significant — average a 3×3 cell window before drawing inference.

dimensions.name: VV
dimensions.description: Vertical-transmit, vertical-receive gamma-naught backscatter (currently materialized).

dimensions.name: VH
dimensions.description: Vertical-transmit, horizontal-receive backscatter — reserved slot, not yet materialized.

bands._source_note: Cube reserves 2 fp16 slots: [0]=VV dB, [1]=VH dB. VH attestation is forward-compatible — a future materializer can attest [VV, VH] without shifting any subsequent offset.

bands.description: Compact terrain triple from a coarse DEM source (legacy 30 m DEM stack). For the canonical Copernicus DEM stats use `cop_dem` instead.
bands.interpretation: Used by older algorithms that pre-date the wider `cop_dem` slot. New consumers should prefer `cop_dem` + `terrain_derived`.

bands.description: ESA WorldCover 2021 v200 class-frequency vector at 10 m. Eight slots carry the per-class fractional cover inside the cell.
bands.interpretation: Slots correspond to the WorldCover legend (tree, shrub, grass, crop, built, bare, snow/ice, water). A cell with `built > 0.4` is generally urban; `crop > 0.6` is agricultural; `tree > 0.5` is forested. Sum across slots ≤ 1.0 (some cells include unmapped fragments).
bands.pitfalls: WorldCover is a single-epoch product (2021). For change use `forest_change` (Hansen) or compare two `geotessera` vintages.

dimensions.name: tree
dimensions.description: ESA WorldCover class 10 (tree cover) fractional area inside cell.

dimensions.name: shrub
dimensions.description: ESA WorldCover class 20 (shrubland) fractional area.

dimensions.name: grass
dimensions.description: ESA WorldCover class 30 (grassland) fractional area.

dimensions.name: crop
dimensions.description: ESA WorldCover class 40 (cropland) fractional area.

dimensions.name: built
dimensions.description: ESA WorldCover class 50 (built-up) fractional area.

dimensions.name: bare
dimensions.description: ESA WorldCover class 60 (bare / sparse vegetation) fractional area.

dimensions.name: snow_ice
dimensions.description: ESA WorldCover class 70 (snow / ice) fractional area.

dimensions.name: water
dimensions.description: ESA WorldCover class 80 (permanent water) fractional area.

bands.description: Compact current-weather quad sourced from Open-Meteo / MET Norway forecast blends. Four scalars covering temperature, cloud, precipitation, and wind.
bands.interpretation: Current-state climate the agent can fold into algorithms like `heat_index@1` or `evapotranspiration_fao56@1`. For climatology / historical fields use `terraclimate` or `koppen`.
bands.pitfalls: Tempo is `medium` because forecasts are refreshed hourly; recall for old tslots returns the historical reanalysis if the upstream supports it, otherwise Absence.

dimensions.name: temperature_2m
dimensions.description: Air temperature at 2 m above ground.

dimensions.name: cloud_cover
dimensions.description: Total cloud cover (0 = clear sky, 100 = fully overcast).

dimensions.name: precipitation_mm
dimensions.description: Precipitation accumulated over the current tslot.

dimensions.name: wind_speed_10m
dimensions.description: Wind speed at 10 m above ground.

bands.description: Three optical-index scalars derived from Sentinel-2 L2A: NDVI, NDRE, NDMI. The most-pulled vegetation surface — these are what algorithms like `flood_risk@2`, `crop_stress@1`, and `irrigation_demand@1` compose.
bands.interpretation: NDVI < 0 = water / snow; 0 – 0.2 = bare soil / urban; 0.2 – 0.5 = sparse / stressed vegetation; 0.5 – 0.8 = healthy crops / shrub; > 0.8 = dense closed canopy. NDMI tracks canopy water content (negative = dry, positive = moist). NDRE is more sensitive to chlorophyll than NDVI and saturates later — preferred for in-season nitrogen monitoring.
bands.pitfalls: Cloud / shadow flips signs misleadingly — always co-check `weather.cloud_cover` and the scene's recorded `cloud_pct` in the receipt. NDVI saturates above ~0.85 (use NDRE / `geotessera` for dense canopies).

dimensions.name: ndvi
dimensions.description: Normalised Difference Vegetation Index

dimensions.name: ndre
dimensions.description: Normalised Difference Red-Edge — chlorophyll-sensitive, less prone to saturation than NDVI

dimensions.name: ndmi
dimensions.description: Normalised Difference Moisture Index — canopy water content

bands.description: Position-encoded Fourier features of the cell's lat/lng. Lets a model condition on absolute location without losing translational structure.
bands.interpretation: Treat as opaque positional input to downstream learned models. Not human-readable per-dimension.

bands.description: Fourier features over `tslot` (day-of-year × year). Encodes seasonality and yearly drift in a way a downstream model can pick up without learning a calendar.
bands.interpretation: Opaque positional input. Pair with `spatial_fourier` for full (where, when) conditioning.

bands.description: Segment Anything 3 visual embedding distilled per cell. Captures fine-grained segmentation-aware structure (object boundaries, texture).
bands.interpretation: Opaque per-dim. Distance metric: cosine. Privacy class is `l2_only_with_model_cid` because raw access could leak the upstream image; agents must quote the model CID alongside any derived fact.
bands.pitfalls: Model-version-locked: a vector minted under SAM3 v1.0 is not directly comparable to one minted under v1.1. Always carry the model CID.

bands.description: Qwen-VL visual embedding distilled per cell. Captures semantic / scene-graph structure (what the cell *looks like* at a high level).
bands.interpretation: Opaque per-dim. Compare via cosine. Same privacy + model-CID rules as `sam3_visual`.

bands.description: Higher-order topographic features derived from the DEM stack: slope statistics, curvature, aspect histograms, TPI / TRI, roughness.
bands.interpretation: Used by algorithms like `landslide_susceptibility@1` and `hydrological_routing@1`. Slope / aspect are circular variables — never average them naively across the slot range; use the canonical formulas.
bands.references: Wilson & Gallant 2000 (Terrain Analysis: Principles and Applications)

bands.description: Temporal-difference encoding of vegetation state across the year — captures rate of change rather than magnitude. Useful for distinguishing seasonal cycling from sustained loss / gain.
bands.interpretation: A cell with high temporal_diff variance is rapidly changing (e.g. logging, planting, flood); near-zero variance is stable forest / desert / urban. Pair with `phenology` to disambiguate.
bands.pitfalls: Cloud-induced gaps in the underlying NDVI series can spike spurious deltas. Tessera's quality mask is applied upstream but the residual floor is non-zero.

bands.description: Per-cell phenology summary: start-of-season (SOS), peak-of-season (POS), end-of-season (EOS), amplitude, plus cycle-shape parameters.
bands.units: mixed (day-of-year, NDVI amplitude, dimensionless shape parameters)
bands.interpretation: Use SOS/EOS to identify cropping calendars (single, double, triple cropping), distinguish evergreen vs deciduous forest, and detect phenology shift year-on-year (climate-change signal).
bands.pitfalls: Equatorial / tropical cells have weak seasonal signal — the cycle-shape parameters degenerate and POS is poorly defined. Treat amplitudes < 0.1 as 'no clear season'.
bands.references: Zhang et al. 2003 (MODIS Land Surface Phenology)

bands.description: Graph-based local-neighbourhood features: connectivity to roads, distance-to-river decay, watershed-membership encoding.
bands.units: mixed (distances in metres, normalised connectivity scores)
bands.interpretation: Lets algorithms condition on infrastructure connectivity (e.g. accessibility, hydrological flow direction) without re-routing OSRM each call.

bands.description: Multi-scale aggregate of NDVI / EVI statistics over progressively larger neighbourhoods (10 m, 30 m, 90 m, 270 m, 810 m, 2.43 km).
bands.interpretation: Compare a cell's NDVI to its surroundings at different scales — picks up edge effects (forest/clearing boundary), small fields embedded in large landscapes, or anomaly hotspots.

bands.description: Annual nightlight composite at the cell (DMSP-OLS V4 intercalibrated stable lights, 1992-2013). VIIRS DNB v22 was the originally-targeted source but EOG/CSM moved its archive behind OAuth in 2024-2025; the wired open-data path is NOAA NCEI NGDC, the only globally-mirrored, anonymous, Range-readable annual nightlight time series.
bands.units: see scalar_keys (avg_dn is uint8 0..63, year is uint16, satellite is string)
bands.interpretation: Strong proxy for human activity intensity at night — picks up settlements down to ~hamlet scale. A drop year-over-year often signals out-migration, conflict, or grid failure; a spike signals new development. Comparing DMSP-OLS DN to VIIRS DNB radiance requires the Li & Zhou 2017 (Remote Sensing 9:637) intercalibration regression — DN is not the same physical unit as nW · cm⁻² · sr⁻¹.
bands.pitfalls: Snow on roofs reflects moonlight and inflates DN in winter at high latitudes. The intercalibration mask zeroes ephemeral lights (fires, gas flares); a 0 at this cell genuinely means 'no measurable persistent night-time emission' and IS a Primary fact (not Absence). Privacy: aggregate_only at min_res 11; finer recall is denied. Coverage: -65°S to 75°N (DMSP-OLS V4 raster bounds).
bands.references: Elvidge et al. 1997 (DMSP-OLS); Li & Zhou 2017 (Remote Sensing 9:637, DN-to-radiance regression)

bands.description: Global Human Settlement Layer (GHSL) features: built-up surface, built-up volume, settlement classification (rural / suburban / urban centre).
bands.units: mixed (m² of built surface, m³ of built volume, integer class codes)
bands.interpretation: GHSL is the most globally consistent built-environment product — use for cross-country urbanisation comparisons where Overture coverage is uneven. The GHS-SMOD code answers 'is this an urban centre / cluster / rural?' directly.
bands.pitfalls: Built-up *surface* counts roof area, not population. A warehouse cluster scores very high without housing anyone. Always cross-check with `population` or `nightlights`.

bands.description: WorldPop unconstrained 100 m population estimate plus density quantiles inside the cell. Eight slots: count, density mean, density p50, p90, dependency-ratio proxy, Δ-since-2000, urban-fraction, rural-fraction.
bands.interpretation: Use for exposure analyses (heat-stress × population, flood × population). Pairs naturally with `flood_risk@2` to compute population-weighted hazard.
bands.pitfalls: WorldPop is a *modelled* dasymetric — it spreads census totals using building footprints + nightlights as priors. Counts are not census-accurate at the per-cell level; aggregate to admin-2 or coarser before quoting.

bands.description: Hansen Global Forest Change v1.11 features: tree-cover-2000 percentage, year-of-loss, year-of-gain, plus annualised loss-rate summaries.
bands.units: mixed (percent canopy cover, year [2001..present], hectares lost / year)
bands.interpretation: `year_of_loss > 0` flags pixels that lost canopy since 2001 — the canonical deforestation signal. Pair with `mangrove` or `protected` to score 'illegal deforestation in a protected area'.
bands.pitfalls: Hansen counts canopy cover, not necessarily *primary forest*. Plantation harvest reads as 'loss' the same as primary deforestation. Use `mangrove` and ecoregion masks to disambiguate.
bands.references: Hansen et al. 2013 (Science) + annual updates at https://glad.umd.edu/dataset/global-2010-tree-cover-30-m

bands.description: Global Mangrove Watch v3 features: mangrove fraction, year-of-establishment, year-of-loss, biomass proxy.
bands.interpretation: Critical for blue-carbon accounting and coastal-protection algorithms. Combine with `forest_change` to detect mangrove-specific loss.

bands.description: World Database on Protected Areas (WDPA) features: any-overlap flag, IUCN-category code, designation year, area-fraction inside cell.
bands.interpretation: Lets risk algorithms uplift cells inside national parks, biosphere reserves, etc. The any-overlap flag is the cheap gate; IUCN category (Ia, Ib, II..VI) carries the strictness.

bands.description: JRC Global Surface Water v1.4 features at 30 m: recurrence, occurrence, seasonality, transitions (1984–2021 climatology) plus current-year occurrence updates.
bands.interpretation: `recurrence` is the canonical signed answer to 'has this cell historically been wet?' — 0 = never water, 100 = water every year of the record. `occurrence` adds current-year updates. Use for flood-baseline reasoning, not real-time inundation (for that use Sentinel-1 + radar water).
bands.pitfalls: Bounded ±60° latitude (JRC's observation footprint). Polar regions return Absence. Glacier and turbid-river cells can score high recurrence without being navigable open water.

bands.description: Ocean-colour chlorophyll-a concentration and SST anomaly summary from Copernicus Marine / NASA OceanColor.
bands.interpretation: Coastal cells: high chl-a + warm SST anomaly = harmful-algal-bloom risk. Open-ocean cells: chl-a tracks productivity / fisheries.
bands.pitfalls: Ocean-only — recall over land returns Absence. Cloud-masked daily product; use 8-day composites for stable trend reading.

bands.description: Köppen-Geiger climate classification at 1 km, expanded to a 32-D one-hot + neighbourhood-blend vector.
bands.interpretation: Use for cross-region transfer reasoning ('this Tropical-Wet cell behaves like that Tropical-Wet cell'). The neighbourhood blend captures climate ecotones (transitions between classes).

bands.description: TerraClimate monthly climatology: temperature (min/max/mean), precipitation, PET, soil moisture, runoff, climate water deficit, plus 30-year normals and recent-decade anomalies.
bands.interpretation: The canonical climatology for drought (climate water deficit, soil moisture), water balance (PET vs precip), and bioclimatic envelope work. Tempo `medium` because monthly updates lag ~2 months.

bands.description: Copernicus GLO-30 DEM statistics at the cell: elevation mean, percentiles, slope, aspect (sin/cos), TRI roughness.
bands.interpretation: Default DEM source for emem — superior to SRTM at high latitudes and over dense canopy. Use for hydro-routing, viewshed, slope-stability, and sea-level-rise exposure.
bands.pitfalls: GLO-30 is a *surface* model (DSM): it includes building / canopy heights. Subtract `landcover.tree` × typical canopy height for a bare-earth approximation, or pull GLO-90 for a DTM-like product.

bands.description: ISRIC SoilGrids 2.0 features at four standard depths (0–5, 5–15, 15–30, 30–60 cm): SOC, sand, silt, clay, bulk density, pH.
bands.interpretation: Used by `soil_carbon_baseline@1`, `crop_suitability@1`, and `infiltration_capacity@1`. SOC at 0–30 cm is the standard for carbon-credit baselines.
bands.pitfalls: SoilGrids is a *predictive map* with R² ~0.4–0.6 — use for relative ranking, not absolute soil-survey accuracy. Coarse 250 m native; downsampling to 10 m is interpolated.

bands.description: WWF terrestrial ecoregions one-hot blended over the cell: biome class, ecoregion ID, conservation status, plus neighbourhood mixing.
bands.interpretation: Lets algorithms condition on biogeographic context — same `cop_dem.elevation_mean` means different things in Amazonia vs the Tibetan Plateau.

bands.description: GADM / Natural Earth administrative-boundary encoding: country (ISO3 hash), admin-1, admin-2, urban/rural classification.
bands.interpretation: Cheap way to filter recall by jurisdiction without round-tripping a polygon-in-polygon test.

bands.description: Tail reservation slot for future cube extensions. Currently zero-padded — recall returns Absence.
bands.interpretation: Do not query. Reserved for future band families without breaking byte-stable layout.
